title: Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur
tagline: Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kanthaswamy Temple in Cheyyur, in Chengalpattu district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Murugan.
overview.description: Kanthaswamy Temple in Cheyyur, in Chengalpattu district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Murugan. Constructed in the ancient Tamil style of architecture, the temple was constructed during the Cholas during the 10th century. Murugan is worshipped as Kanthaswamy along with his consorts Valli and Deivasena. A granite wall surrounds the temple, enclosing all its shrines.

topPlaces.name: Edaikazhinadu
topPlaces.description: Edaikazhinadu is a panchayat town in Chengalpattu district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. Nearby villages are Vennangupattu and Kadappakkam.

topPlaces.name: Mudaliarkuppam Boat House
topPlaces.description: Mudaliarkuppam Boat House (Tamil: முதலியார்குப்பம் படகுக் குழாம்), also known as Raindrop Boat House, is a water sport facility located on the East Coast Road, 36 km to the south of Mamallapuram and 92 km from Chennai in India. It was developed by the Tamil Nadu Tourism Development Corporation on the Odiyur lake back waters.

topPlaces.name: Cheyyur taluk
topPlaces.description: Cheyyur taluk is a taluk of Chengalpattu district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Cheyyur.

topPlaces.name: Cheyyur
topPlaces.description: Cheyyur (Tamil: [sɛjjuːr]) is a town in Chengalpattu district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Cheyyur is surrounded by four sub-areas: North, East, South and West Cheyyur.

topPlaces.name: Endiyur
topPlaces.description: Endiyur is a village panchayat located in Tindivanam, in the Villupuram district of Tamil-Nadu, India. The native language of the village is Tamil.

topPlaces.name: Onampakkam
topPlaces.description: Onampakkam is a small village in Maduranthakam taluk in Chengalpattu district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The major occupation of the people living at this place is agriculture.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (73 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (85 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur?
faq.a: Stays in Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kanthaswamy Temple, Cheyyur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Edaikazhinadu, Mudaliarkuppam Boat House, Cheyyur taluk, Cheyyur, Endiyur.
